output:
CVE-2021-1066 describes a medium-severity vulnerability found in NVIDIA's vGPU manager plugin related to improper input validation (CWE-20). Specifically, this flaw can lead to unexpected resource consumption, resulting in a denial of service (DoS). It affects versions 8.x (prior to 8.6) and 11.0 (prior to 11.3) of the vGPU manager. This issue is associated with the MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1499, which relates to DoS via resource exhaustion.

Administrators should mitigate this vulnerability by updating to the latest secure versions of the vGPU manager. Versions earlier than 8.6 and 11.3 should be specifically prioritized for update to prevent potential service disruptions. Furthermore, integrating best practices for input data validation in software management can help prevent similar issues in the future.
